brief_justification: Paper A recasts Fermionic Monte Carlo estimation as Bayesian statistical inference — learning permutation-family probabilities/energies with physical priors and an LSTM residual model plus active importance sampling for variance reduction — while paper B gives an algebraic-geometry description of Coulomb-branch symplectic singularities via toric blowups and a classification of symplectic leaves and transverse slices. The only overlap is that both mention quantum/gauge physics as motivation; their machinery (probabilistic inference and Monte Carlo variance reduction vs. symplectic geometry of singular varieties) is entirely different, so no role-for-role mapping holds.
